title: Defer
description: The #[Defer] attribute makes a component load immediately after the initial page load is complete, preventing slow components from blocking the page render.

sections.title: #Basic usage
sections.content:
Apply the #[Defer] attribute to any component that should be deferred: With #[Defer], the component initially renders as an empty
, then loads immediately after the page finishes loading—without waiting for it to enter the viewport.

examples.code:
amount = Transaction::monthToDate()->sum('amount'); }};?> 
Revenue this month: {{ $amount }}

sections.title: #Lazy vs Defer
sections.content:
Livewire provides two ways to delay component loading:
Both prevent slow components from blocking your initial page render, but differ in when the component actually loads.

sections.title: #Rendering placeholders
sections.content:
By default, Livewire renders an empty
before the component loads. You can provide a custom placeholder using the placeholder() method: Users will see the loading spinner until the component fully loads. If your placeholder's root element is a
, your component must also use a
element.

examples.code:
amount = Transaction::monthToDate()->sum('amount'); }  public function placeholder() { return <<<'HTML'
HTML; } };?> 
Revenue this month: {{ $amount }}

sections.title: #Bundling requests
sections.content:
By default, deferred components load in parallel with independent network requests. To bundle multiple deferred components into a single request, use the bundle parameter:
Now, if there are ten revenue components on the page, all ten will load via a single bundled network request instead of ten parallel requests.

examples.code: <?php // resources/views/components/⚡revenue.blade.php use Livewire\Attributes\Defer;use Livewire\Component; new #[Defer(bundle: true)] class extends Component { // ...};

sections.title: #Alternative approach
sections.content:
Instead of the attribute, you can defer specific component instances using the defer parameter:
This is useful when you only want certain instances of a component to be deferred.
If a component has #[Defer] but you want to load it immediately in certain cases, you can override it:

sections.title: #Learn more
sections.content: For complete documentation on lazy and deferred loading, including placeholders and bundling strategies, see the Lazy Loading documentation.
